title: ci-log-summary.v1
description: Structured failure summary of a single CI log, written by the ci-log-reducer skill to .laguna/ci-log-summary.json at the workspace root. Every cited line must exist in the analyzed log and carry its verbatim text; the validator (scripts/validate_log_summary.ts) enforces this mechanically.

schema_version.description: Contract identifier; always the literal string "ci-log-summary.v1".

log_file.description: Workspace-relative path of the CI log that was analyzed (e.g. "ci.log" or "logs/ci-run-4182.log"). Must resolve to a file inside the workspace.

failing_command.description: The command that actually failed. Must be supported by evidence: it appears in the log itself or in the CI job metadata (ci-job.json), never invented.

summary.description: What failed and why, in plain language. Covers every distinct failure when there are several.

error_lines.description: The decisive error lines, cited from the log. Each entry pairs a 1-based line number with the verbatim text of that line.

text.description: The exact text of that line, copied verbatim from the log (trailing whitespace insignificant).

suggested_next_commands.description: Safe, local commands a developer should run next to reproduce or narrow the failure. No network access (installs, fetches, pushes) and nothing destructive (deletions, hard resets, sudo).
